findings.evidence: `TauCeti/Analysis/InnerProductSpace/Variational.lean:31` assumes `[InnerProductSpace ℝ V]`, while `mathlib/Mathlib/Analysis/Normed/Operator/NormedSpace.lean:293` defines `IsCoercive` for `[SeminormedAddCommGroup E] [NormedSpace ℝ E]`; the proofs at lines 39-46 and 52-55 use only coercivity, norm algebra, and linearity, not the inner product.
findings.fix: Move these lemmas to a normed-space section, e.g. `variable {V} [NormedAddCommGroup V] [NormedSpace ℝ V] {B : V →L[ℝ] V →L[ℝ] ℝ}`, and keep `[InnerProductSpace ℝ V] [CompleteSpace V]` only for the Riesz/Lax-Milgram solution API.
findings.issue: `eq_zero_of_forall_apply_eq_zero` and `eq_of_forall_apply_eq_of_forall_apply_eq` are uniqueness consequences of coercivity but are stated under `[InnerProductSpace ℝ V]` from line 31.

summary: The existence and solution-operator API sits at the natural real Hilbert-space level, but the two uniqueness-only lemmas still carry an unnecessary inner-product assumption.
